examples.title: Queue Array Implementation
examples.description: Basic queue implementation using fixed-size array with enqueue and dequeue operations
examples.code:
STRUCTURE Queue
  ARRAY data[MAXSIZE]
  INTEGER front = -1
  INTEGER rear = -1
END STRUCTURE

FUNCTION isEmpty(queue)
  IF queue.front == -1 THEN
    RETURN TRUE
  ELSE
    RETURN FALSE
  END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION isFull(queue)
  IF queue.rear == MAXSIZE - 1 THEN
    RETURN TRUE
  ELSE
    RETURN FALSE
  END IF
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION enqueue(queue, item)
  IF isFull(queue) THEN
    PRINT "Queue Overflow"
    RETURN
  END IF
  IF queue.front == -1 THEN
    queue.front = 0
  END IF
  queue.rear = queue.rear + 1
  queue.data[queue.rear] = item
END FUNCTION

FUNCTION dequeue(queue)
  IF isEmpty(queue) THEN
    PRINT "Queue Underflow"
    RETURN NULL
  END IF
  item = queue.data[queue.front]
  IF queue.front == queue.rear THEN
    queue.front = -1
    queue.rear = -1
  ELSE
    queue.front = queue.front + 1
  END IF
  RETURN item
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows array-based queue implementation with front and rear pointers. The enqueue operation adds at rear, dequeue removes from front. It includes overflow and underflow checks.

examples.title: Circular Queue in C
examples.description: Circular queue implementation using modulo operator to wrap around
examples.explanation: This C program implements circular queue using modulo operator (%). The key advantage is that when rear reaches MAXSIZE-1, it wraps to 0, allowing reuse of dequeued space. One slot is kept empty to distinguish full from empty state.

examples.title: Queue Using List in Python
examples.description: Simple queue implementation using Python list with built-in operations
examples.explanation: This Python implementation uses list as underlying storage. The enqueue uses append() and dequeue adjusts front pointer. Python's list handles dynamic memory but in practice, collections.deque is more efficient for queue operations.

examples.title: Queue Using Linked List in Java
examples.description: Queue implementation using linked list for dynamic size
examples.explanation: This Java implementation uses linked list for queue, allowing dynamic size without capacity limitations. New nodes are added at rear and removed from front. No overflow except when system runs out of memory.
